search:kubernetes+redis-cache:kubernetes.content:
- Use Helm for deploying Redis on Kubernetes.
- Install Helm using `curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/helm/helm/main/scripts/get-helm-3 | bash`.
- Deploy Redis with Helm using `helm install my-redis bitnami/redis --set architecture=standalone`.
- Check the status of your deployment with `kubectl get pods`.
- Access the Redis server by running `kubectl run -i --tty --rm debug --image=busybox --restart=Never -- nslookup my-redis-master`.
- Key config options include `replicaCount`, `password`, and `persistence.enabled` for data persistence.
- Ensure Kubernetes version compatibility with Helm charts, typically using the latest stable versions of both.

- Use `kubectl scale` to horizontally scale Redis deployments.
- Configure Redis using ConfigMaps and Secrets.
- Deploy Redis Operator for automated management of Redis clusters on Kubernetes.
- Ensure at least two worker nodes in your cluster for high availability.
- Monitor Redis performance with Prometheus and Grafana.
- Update Redis version through Helm charts if applicable.
